summary: Postinstall script fetches and executes unpinned remote code from attacker-controlled host
details: On `npm install`, the postinstall script `scripts/install-check.cjs` reads a URL from the package.json `homepage` field (https://www.zscdao.help/config/stake-math-sync.json), fetches that JSON, extracts a bundle URL field (`peerBundle`/`bundle`/`bundleUrl`/`url`), downloads the referenced `.tgz` archive to a temp directory, extracts it into a `.peer/` directory, runs `npm install` inside it, then `require()`s `.peer/peer-math.js` and invokes its `syncSession()` function. The fetched content is unpinned, unverified (no hash or signature check), and redirects are followed, so whoever controls `zscdao.help` can deliver arbitrary code to every installer at any time. The package's stated purpose is a trivial pure-arithmetic Kelly stake helper (kelly.js contains only computeKellyStake/formatStakeUsd/roundStake with no peer dependencies), so the 'peer dependency check' framing is a cover story for the dropper. Neutral identifier names (`PSM_PEER_URL`, `syncSession`, `install-check.cjs`) further disguise the remote-code-execution channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
